Ad Text:
Lead Business Analyst
Location: Subiaco | Full-Time 8–9-month temp contract with potential extension

About the Role
We’re looking for an experienced Lead Business Analyst to play a key role in shaping and delivering strategic initiatives within a complex and evolving environment. This is a senior position where you’ll lead business analysis activities, influence decision-making, and drive outcomes that deliver real value across the organisation.
You’ll work closely with stakeholders across all levels to translate business needs into actionable solutions, while contributing to long-term strategic objectives and continuous improvement initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Drive the successful delivery of business initiatives by translating business needs, quantifying impacts, and supporting implementation and integration activities
Lead end-to-end business requirements gathering, including planning, analysis, documentation, communication, and validation
Develop and contribute to strategic roadmaps supporting incremental delivery of long-term objectives
Facilitate workshops, meetings, and stakeholder engagement sessions to identify needs, clarify objectives, and generate innovative solutions
Conduct high-level research and analysis of global trends, emerging technologies, and industry innovation to inform business strategy
Provide expert advice and guidance using strategic business analysis techniques to support initiative planning, process reviews, and solution design
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to scope and develop concept proposals and feasibility assessments
Analyse complex business problems and deliver insights and recommendations to drive effective solutions
Build and maintain strong relationships with a broad range of stakeholders, including suppliers, consultants, and industry partners
Champion and embed continuous improvement practices within a collaborative, multi-disciplinary team
Contribute to a high-performance culture through leadership, innovation, and best practice methodologies
About You
You are a seasoned Business Analyst with a strategic mindset and a strong ability to influence outcomes in complex environments.
Essential Criteria

Proven experience as a Business Analyst within a mid-tier or complex organisation
Demonstrated expertise in applying strategic business analysis methodologies to solve business problems and deliver value
Strong experience evaluating project and program performance, driving continuous improvement, and fostering high-performing teams
Exceptional communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to build relationships across all levels
Highly developed analytical, conceptual, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complexity into clear recommendations
Why Join Us?

Opportunity to lead impactful, organisation-wide initiatives
Work in a collaborative and forward-thinking environment
Influence strategic direction and continuous improvement
Engage with diverse stakeholders and cutting-edge ideas
Interviews to commence Immediatley. Please note only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

We’re partnering with a leading Australian Bank to assist them in their search for several Senior Performance Leads. This is a fantastic opportunity to support major transformations and technology portfolios across digital, customer and business domains.
 
This role is ideal for candidates from a management consulting, strategy or  performance background who enjoy working closely with senior stakeholders to drive business performance, governance and strategic delivery outcomes. You will act as a trusted partner and advisor to your Technology and General Managers, working with business delivery teams to provide effective delivery oversight, manage governance rhythms and cadences, work with senior leadership to define and manage objectives and key results (OKRs).
 
Key responsibilities include:

Driving quarterly planning and portfolio governance
Delivering performance reporting and strategic insights
Supporting investment, workforce and financial decision-making
Improving delivery oversight, operating rhythms and execution
Facilitating executive forums and stakeholder engagement
 
We’re looking for:

Experience in consulting, transformation, strategy or portfolio governance
Strong commercial and analytical capability
Executive stakeholder management experience
Exposure to agile delivery, OKRs and enterprise programs
Excellent communication and reporting skills
 
Financial services experience is highly regarded.

Are you a finance professional with a passion for delivering insightful reporting and driving business performance? SA Power Networks is seeking a Senior Planning and Reporting Analyst to join our team and play a key role in shaping the financial future of our organisation.

 

About the role

As a Senior Analyst in the Planning and Reporting team, you will be responsible for delivering accurate, timely, and insightful financial performance reporting, budgeting, and forecasting for SA Power Networks. You will work closely with key stakeholders across the business to ensure strong financial governance, identify risks and opportunities, and provide data-led insights to support decision-making.

This role offers the opportunity to manage the end-to-end financial management of capital and operating portfolios, both for specific business areas and individual projects. You will also contribute to the development and enhancement of financial systems and processes, driving efficiencies and improvements across the organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Drive a culture focused on Performance by delivering analysis and insights through monthly performance reporting, supporting Customer & Strategy and the Corporate business units.
Coordinate and prepare annual capital and operating budgets, partnering with business leaders to drive accountability and commercial acumen.
Manage the forecasting process to ensure proactive financial performance management and early identification of risks and opportunities.
Collaborate with Business Relationship Management and key operational stakeholders to deliver value-add financial processes and insights.
Play a pivotal role in ensuring reporting is fit-for-purpose (Power BI) and processes used to deliver objectives are efficient and effective.
Support the Planning and Reporting Manager and the wider FP&A team in achieving commercial and strategic objectives.

 

Culture & benefits

At SA Power Networks, you’ll be part of a team where our values shape how we work every day.

We keep everyone safe, collaborate with purpose, take the initiative, and rise to the challenge to deliver for our people, customers and communities.



We offer an environment that values safety above all, encourages new ideas, and supports you to grow your career while contributing to something meaningful for South Australia.

 

You will also enjoy:



A commitment to work-life balance with truly flexible working arrangements, a great team environment and a career focus with development opportunities.
13.5% superannuation (1% above current legislated employer contributions)
Up to $900 in electricity reimbursements each year
17.5% leave loading payable on annual leave
Automatic coverage from SA Power Networks income protection scheme
Paid parental leave (5 weeks) and maternity leave (18 weeks) 
An organisation committed to the safety and wellbeing of its employees
Corporate discounts on health insurance and gym memberships

Our salaries are positioned well above industry standards; with this role offering a starting annual base salary of $147,824.

 

About You
To succeed in this role, you will have:

Strong experience in financial planning, reporting, and analysis, ideally within a complex organisation.
A proven ability to deliver accurate and timely financial insights that drive business performance.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build collaborative relationships and influence decision-making.
A proactive approach to identifying risks and opportunities and implementing solutions.
Strong technical skills in financial systems and reporting tools, with a focus on process improvement.
A relevant tertiary qualification in finance, accounting, or a related field (CPA/CA qualification is highly regarded).

 

About us
SA Power Networks plays a vital role in South Australia, connecting homes, businesses and communities to a safe, reliable and efficient electricity network.

We’re proud to be leading the transition to a cleaner, more sustainable energy future working closely with communities across the state to deliver meaningful outcomes today and for generations to come.

Our people are at the heart of everything we do. With a collaborative culture, strong development pathways and a commitment to innovation, you’ll be supported to grow your career while making a real impact, connecting South Australians to a better energy future, today.



Want to know more?
For queries only contact chr•••••••••••••@sapowernetworks.com.au

Applications (Cover Letter and Resume) should be addressed to Christina Lekkas.

Diversity & inclusion

SA Power Networks is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We encourage applications from all backgrounds, cultures and experiences.

Our client is proudly an Australian customer engagement and payments technology company that has been helping leading brands connect with customers for more than 25 years.

Operating across rewards, loyalty, incentives, prepaid payments, gift cards, promotions and data analytics, the Group delivers innovative technology solutions that power customer engagement at scale.

As a Principal Member of Visa with an Australian issuing licence, the business issues prepaid cards directly, strengthening its position in the rapidly growing payments and loyalty market. Following continued growth through both acquisition and organic expansion, an opportunity now exists for a commercially minded finance leader to join the executive finance team.

The Opportunity

Reporting directly to the CFO, this is a senior, hands-on Commercial Finance Business Partner role focused on improving commercial performance across the Group.

This position is built around the philosophy of “Challenge & Involve” — partnering closely with divisional leaders to challenge assumptions, provide commercial insight and improve decision-making across the business.

You will play a critical role in budgeting, forecasting, commercial modelling and divisional performance analysis while helping elevate the broader finance function.

This role suits a commercially astute finance professional who enjoys partnering with the business, influencing outcomes and working in a fast-moving growth environment.

Key Responsibilities

Commercial Finance & Business Partnering

Own and continuously improve divisional P&Ls, including revenue recognition, margin reporting and cost allocations

Lead annual budgeting and rolling forecast processes across multiple business units

Deliver insightful monthly variance analysis, identifying performance drivers and improvement opportunities

Build and maintain commercial models to support pricing, investment, customer and operational decisions

Provide scenario analysis and commercial input into business cases and strategic initiatives

Partner closely with divisional and operational leaders to improve commercial decision-making

Support broader finance operations including reporting, cash flow awareness, controls and compliance

Leadership & Continuous Improvement

Work collaboratively with the CFO and Executive Leadership Team

Help develop finance capability, reporting standards and commercial discipline

Improve reporting processes, data visibility and automation opportunities

Contribute to a high-performance, commercially focused finance culture

About You

You are commercially curious, analytical and confident engaging with senior stakeholders across the business. You bring strong financial discipline while also understanding how to influence operational and commercial outcomes.

Essential Experience

CA or CPA qualified

Degree qualified in Accounting, Commerce, Finance or related discipline

5–7 years’ post qualification experience in a Financial Controller, Finance Manager or Commercial Finance Business Partner role

Strong hands-on experience across budgeting, forecasting, divisional P&L management and commercial modelling

Experience leading and developing finance teams

Advanced Excel capability and exposure to ERP systems

Highly Regarded

Experience within payments, fintech, prepaid cards, loyalty or customer engagement sectors

Experience working within a multi-entity or group structure

Knowledge of Australian Accounting Standards, tax obligations and statutory reporting

Exposure to Power BI, analytics tools or enterprise data environments

Experience in a high-growth SME or mid-market environment

What’s On Offer

Opportunity to partner directly with an experienced CFO and executive leadership team

Exposure to a growing fintech and payments environment

Highly commercial role with broad influence across the business

Supportive, entrepreneurial and growth-focused culture

Melbourne-based opportunity with flexibility

If you are looking for a commercially impactful finance role where you can genuinely influence business performance and strategy, we would love to hear from you.

We are seeking an experienced Data Architect to lead and grow enterprise data architecture capabilities, with an immediate focus on supporting a major ERP transformation and a longer-term mandate to establish strong, scalable data foundations across the organisation.

Key responsibilities: 

Lead data architecture across a major ERP transformation program
Define data standards, governance, models, and data quality frameworks
Own data migration scope, impacts, and cross-system dependencies
Ensure data consistency across ERP, analytics, and enterprise systems
Guide data integration, platform design, and transformation patterns
Establish enterprise data architecture capability and best practices
Partner with business, IT, and vendors to align on data solutions
Support delivery through testing, validation, and data integrity controls
Key requirements: 

Essential
7-10+ years’ experience in data-focused roles
Proven experience delivering data architecture in complex enterprise environments
Experience with large-scale data migration, integration, and reporting programs
Strong data modelling, governance, and data quality experience
Experience with modern data platforms (warehouse, ETL/ELT, integration layers)
Ability to define and manage data impacts across multiple systems
Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders
Experience working with vendors, system integrators, and delivery partners
Experience in retail / FMCG, or consumer-facing industries - desirable
Experience in organisations with mature data and analytics capabilities - desirable
Experience establishing data governance frameworks and operating models - desirable
If this position is of interest, click APPLY today, or contact Sam Makdesi on sma•••••@synchropartners.com.au

Emesent is a world leader in autonomous drone technology, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Our solutions are transforming how mining, engineering, infrastructure, and public safety industries capture, understand, and act on spatial data. We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and drive the next generation of our products.


As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the strategy and execution for one or more product lines within Emesent’s portfolio. You’ll work at the intersection of hardware, software, and autonomy defining what we build, why, and how we bring it to market. This is a high-autonomy role where you’ll set direction, make trade-offs, and drive outcomes. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering leads, commercial teams, and users to ensure our products deliver measurable value in complex, real-world environments.

What you’ll do

Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for your area. Translate business objectives, market signals, and user needs into a clear, prioritised plan that balances near-term delivery with longer-term bets

Customer & Market Insight: Deeply understand our users workflows, pain points, and environments. Conduct field visits, user research, and competitive analysis to ensure product decisions are grounded in real-world evidence

Cross-Functional Leadership: Drive alignment across engineering, design, commercial, and client success teams. Drive sprint-level prioritisation within your squads and ensure the team is focused on the highest-impact work

Go-to-Market Execution: Partner with commercial and marketing teams to develop positioning, pricing, and launch strategies. Ensure users and internal teams are well- prepared for every release

Product Analytics: Define and monitor key product metrics. Use quantitative and qualitative data to inform decisions, measure outcomes, and continuously improve the user experience

Stakeholder Communication: Keep leadership and cross-functional stakeholders informed with clear, concise updates on progress, risks, and strategic decisions

What you’ll bring

5+ years of experience in product management, with demonstrated ownership of a product or product area end-to-end

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.

Proven ability to work effectively with engineering teams and translate technical complexity into clear product direction

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across functions and seniority levels

Entrepreneurial mindset, you thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a strong bias for action

Deep empathy for users and a passion for solving real-world problems in complex operating environments

Experience with Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira, Amplitude, or similar

Strongly regarded

Experience in the mining, geospatial, or survey industries

Track record of zero-to-one product development, taking a product from concept through to launch and market traction

Experience building or managing platform software products (APIs, SDKs, data pipelines, or developer-facing tools)

Experience with deep-tech or hardware-integrated products, where product decisions span physical and digital domains

The perks

9-day fortnight - every second Friday off

Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP) share in Emesent’s success

Work alongside a friendly and passionate team building cutting-edge technology

Genuine learning and development opportunities, including access to LinkedIn Learning

Employee Assistance Program

One full day of leave each year for your work anniversary

Novated Lease options

Who are we?

Emesent is a world leader in drone autonomy, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Founded in 2018 with deep roots in Australia’s globally recognised CSIRO, we’ve built a reputation for delivering high-quality spatial data capture across mining, infrastructure, survey, and construction industries. Our flagship product, Hovermap, is an autonomous mobile scanning platform that combines advanced collision avoidance and autonomous flight with LiDAR mapping to capture data in hazardous, GPS-denied, and hard-to-reach environments. Mountable on drones, vehicles, or carried as a backpack, Hovermap has set the standard for mobile mapping in underground mining and confined spaces. Our newest product, GX1, extends Emesent’s mapping technology into the AEC and survey markets. GX1 is a ground-based LiDAR scanner purpose-built for above-ground
environments delivering fast, accurate spatial capture for surveying and reality capture professionals.

With a rapidly growing global customer base and multiple product lines spanning hardware, desktop software, and cloud, Emesent is at an exciting inflection point  and we’re looking for people who want to help shape what comes next.


Why Emesent?
At Emesent, you’ll work on technology that is genuinely shaping the future of how industries capture and understand the physical world. We offer a dynamic, collaborative environment, competitive compensation, and real opportunities for professional growth. Join us in pushing the boundaries of robotics and spatial intelligence.

The Organisation

Think Talent is partnering with a large, purpose-led service organisation delivering complex, high-volume operations across multiple regions. The organisation is currently undertaking a range of business improvement initiatives focused on streamlining processes, improving operational efficiency, and supporting consistent ways of working across teams.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity for an experienced Business BA  to join a fast-paced environment on an initial 3-month contract, supporting key operational and transformation initiatives.

Working closely with business stakeholders, you will analyse current state processes, identify improvement opportunities, facilitate workshops, and support the delivery of practical, scalable business solutions. The role will suit someone who is hands-on, adaptable, and comfortable navigating operational complexity in a large organisation.

Key areas of focus include:
Reviewing and documenting current and future state business processes
Identifying process inefficiencies, gaps and improvement opportunities
Facilitating stakeholder workshops and requirements gathering sessions
Mapping business requirements and translating them into clear documentation
Supporting process improvement and operational change initiatives
Working cross-functionally across operations, technology and business teams
Developing process maps, business cases, workflows and supporting documentation
Assisting with implementation planning and change readiness activities
Supporting consistency and alignment across ways of working

The Right Fit

We are seeking a commercially minded Business Analyst who combines strong process analysis capability with excellent stakeholder engagement skills. You will be comfortable working in dynamic environments, managing competing priorities, and driving clarity across complex operational challenges.

Key capabilities include:
Business Analysis & Process Improvement - Demonstrated experience analysing and improving end-to-end business processes within complex operational environments.
Requirements Gathering & Documentation - Strong capability in facilitating workshops, capturing business requirements, and producing clear, structured documentation including process maps and workflows.
Stakeholder Engagement - Proven ability to build credibility and work collaboratively with a broad range of stakeholders across business and operational teams.
Problem Solving & Delivery Focus - Able to identify practical solutions, simplify complexity, and support initiatives through to implementation.
Adaptability - Comfortable operating in fast-paced environments with shifting priorities and evolving business needs.

The Benefits

This is an opportunity to join a collaborative and purpose-driven organisation undertaking meaningful operational improvement work.

An attractive day rate is on offer, together with flexible ways of working and the opportunity to make an immediate impact within a supportive team environment.

For prompt consideration, click “apply now” or for a confidential discussion, please contact Michelle Mason at Think Talent on 04•• ••• 813.

Finance Business Partner - Customer & Commercial (12 Month Contract)

Since 2011, THE ICONIC has redefined the future of retail in Australia and New Zealand. As the #1 fashion e-commerce & lifestyle destination in the region, our e-commerce platforms provide an inspiring and seamless end-to-end customer experience through our own technology innovations.  

Our Finance team partners across THE ICONIC to turn complex data into clear commercial insights, supporting innovation, tech product launches and strategic decision-making. 

About You..

We’re looking for a commercially minded Finance Business Partner to join our Commercial Finance team and partner closely with senior leaders across our Customer & Commercial functions. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys turning numbers into meaningful business insights, influencing decision-making, and supporting teams to drive revenue, manage costs and improve profitability.

What you’ll do..

Business partner with senior leaders across Customer & Commercial, providing financial support, challenge and insights.

Support annual budget and mid-term planning processes.

Lead the month-end and monthly reporting cycle for Customer & Commercial.

Analyse current and forecasted trade in partnership with Company Planning, with a focus on improving forecast accuracy.

Provide value-add insights across customer, category, promotional and marketing activity performance.

Oversee financial reviews and commercials for large platform deals.

Lead costing reviews for overheads and ensure contracted spend is regularly reviewed and challenged.

Identify risks and opportunities against budget and forecast.

Work with Customer Insights to validate customer builds and performance assumptions.

Oversee people costs across the Customer & Commercial areas.

Play a central role in monthly Customer & Commercial Reviews / MBRs.

Create, improve and automate reports and financial models.

Work closely with Commercial Analysts, Planning and Insights teams to deliver high-quality reporting and analysis.

About you…  

Strong finance business partnering experience with the ability to work with Senior Executives

Professional qualifications such as CA or CPA are highly regarded.

Financial Reporting, Budgeting, Forecasting and Business Case Development & Evaluation

Solid technical accounting and reporting capability

Experience with Tableau or similar analytics/reporting tools.

The ability to translate numbers into a clear business story.

Experience supporting budgeting, forecasting and monthly reporting cycles.

A proactive mindset, with the ability to identify risks, opportunities and performance drivers.

Why you'll love THE ICONIC..

From our ways of working to our growth mindset and sustainable approach, we each add unique value and have an incredible amount of fun doing it! People are our greatest asset and our ICONITE experience is designed to empower you to do your best work. Here’s what to expect when you join THE ICONIC:

Flexible working. We offer a hybrid and flexible working model so you can do your best work in a way that works for you

The learning  collective. Get your knowledge fix with our learning days and hackathons

Parents at THE ICONIC. Access to our parental leave program and an extra day off for your kids' first day of school every year 

Birthday leave. Enjoy a paid day off for your birthday and an ICONIC voucher to treat yourself

Curate your style. No matter what your style is, we have got you covered with our Amazing staff discount and our famous sample sales

People & Planet Positive: From our ethical sourcing and sustainability strategies to our community engagement and diversity, our responsibility is multifaceted, and each of these facets are equally important. 

People  first. Access our Employee Assistance Program for you and your family. 

Volunteer days. Work as a Charity Partner with Thread Together for society and environmental change.

Additional Information

At THE ICONIC, diversity, inclusion, and belonging are integral to our culture, fostering an environment of respect and dignity. We aim to empower self-expression and ensure our workforce reflects our diverse communities. By embracing diverse perspectives and experiences, we strive to create a genuine sense of belonging for our employees, customers, and partners, promoting equal opportunities and a better shopping experience for all

We are committed to providing reasonable arrangements to all individuals participating in our application and interview process, and while performing job functions. If you require any accommodations or adjustments prior to the submission of your application or throughout your interview process, please contact our Talent Acquisition team directly: tal••••••••••••••@theiconic.com.au

Shape enterprise‑wide decision‑making through trusted analytics and insights.
Lead a high‑impact performance analytics function at a Group of Eight university.
Full-time (1.0 FTE) appointment on an ongoing basis.
Base salary range: $134,288 – $142,099 p.a. (pro-rata) plus 17% superannuation.

About the area

You will join the Strategy, Planning and Performance function within the Office of the Chief Financial Officer, the University’s central hub for strategic analysis, institutional planning and performance monitoring. This area supports senior leaders and governance bodies through high‑quality data, analytics and reporting, enabling informed decision‑making across teaching, research, finance and operations.

About the opportunity

Lead the University’s enterprise‑wide performance analytics function, delivering high‑quality data, dashboards and insights that inform executive, governance and regulatory decision‑making.
Partner closely with senior leaders and planning teams to translate complex institutional, sector and market data into clear insights that support strategic planning, budgeting, forecasting and performance monitoring.
Build and uplift analytics capability by setting standards for data governance, analytical methodologies and reporting, while leading and developing a high‑performing team of analytics professionals.

About you

Proven experience leading enterprise‑level performance analytics, reporting or business intelligence functions
Strong capability in data modelling, statistical analysis, forecasting, trend analysis and performance measurement
Experience developing dashboards, reporting products and analytical methodologies that support executive and governance decision‑making
Ability to communicate complex insights clearly and influence senior stakeholders through evidence‑based advice
Excellent people leadership, planning and organisational skills, with experience managing teams and competing priorities

Note: Employment checks will include information on gender-based violence, sexual harassment, and related misconduct to meet legal obligations. A current National Police Clearance will be required by the successful applicant.

Position description:  Manager, Performance Insights PD.pdf

How to apply

Closing date: 11:55 PM AWST on Friday 5th June 2026.

The University of Western Australia (UWA) is ranked among the top 100 universities in the world and a member of the prestigious Australian Group of Eight research intensive universities. With a strong research track record, vibrant campus and working environments, there is no better time to join Western Australia’s top university.

Learn more about us.

Our commitment to inclusion and diversity

UWA is committed to a diverse workforce and an equitable and inclusive workplace. We are committed to fostering a safe environment for all, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, those from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, the LGBTIQA+ community, and people living with disability.

If you have queries relating to your application, please contact the individual named in the advertisement. Alternatively, please contact the Talent team at people@uwa.edu.au with details of your query. To enable a quick response, please include the 6-digit job reference number.

Job Title: Senior Finance Manager – Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)
Ad Text:
Your Impact 

 

We’re looking for a commercially minded and influential finance leader to join our Group Finance team as Senior Finance Manager – FP&A.

You will play a key regional role with responsibility across Oceania (Australia & New Zealand) and South-Central Southeast Asia (SCSEA), providing critical financial insight and planning leadership to support strategic decision-making and business performance.

You will lead APAC financial planning and analysis activities and lead the Group FP&A team, with two direct reports, delivering high-quality consolidated reporting, forecasts, budgets, Mid Term Plan outputs and performance insights. You will play a pivotal role in strengthening planning discipline, improving visibility of performance, and partnering with senior stakeholders to drive outcomes.

 

Key Responsibilities

 

Lead APAC consolidation and performance reporting, delivering timely, accurate and insight-driven outputs for ELT and Board reporting
Own and drive the regional planning cycle, including forecasts, budgets, Mid Term Plan and target-setting processes
Partner with senior leaders across functions and geographies to identify risks, opportunities and actionable insights
Provide balance sheet and cashflow oversight across the region
Continuously improve FP&A processes, governance and capability to enhance decision support
Lead and develop the FP&A team, with accountability for two direct reports, fostering capability, engagement and high performance

 

About You

 

CA/CPA (or equivalent) qualified with strong experience in FP&A, commercial finance or integrated business planning
Proven track record in a senior finance role within a large, complex or matrixed organisation (FMCG experience highly regarded)
Deep experience in budgeting, forecasting, performance reporting and mid-term planning
Strong business acumen with the ability to translate complex financial data into clear, actionable insights
Confident communicator with the ability to influence and challenge senior stakeholders
Demonstrated ability to work across multiple markets, functions and leadership levels
Passionate about continuous improvement, capability uplift and driving better business outcomes

 

Why Asahi Beverages?

 

At Asahi Beverages, we're passionate about crafting more than just drinks - we craft moments of connection, celebration, and shared enjoyment. We bring together a diverse portfolio of iconic beverage brands, a commitment to quality, and a drive to constantly innovate.  Headquartered in Melbourne and with facilities right across Australia and New Zealand, you’ll find Asahi is a place where talent is nurtured, and ambition is rewarded. If you’re ready to toast your future, we’re ready to meet you. 

 

We are a Circle Back Initiative employer and commit to responding to all applicants.

Job Title: Senior Finance Solutions Analyst (12 Month Contract)
Ad Text:
Senior Finance Solutions Analyst (12 Month Contract)

Since 2011, THE ICONIC has redefined the future of retail in Australia and New Zealand. As the #1 fashion e-commerce & lifestyle destination in the region, our e-commerce platforms provide an inspiring and seamless end-to-end customer experience through our own technology innovations.  

Our Commercial Finance team partners across THE ICONIC to turn complex customer and financial data into clear commercial insights, supporting innovation, tech product launches, and strategic decision-making. 

About You..

We’re looking for a commercially minded Senior Finance Solutions Analyst with strong problem-solving skills and a passion for using technology to enhance Finance processes and enable better decision-making. This role will play a key part in transforming how Finance operates, shifting the team away from manual ways of working towards smarter, more automated and insight-led processes.

Working closely with the Finance Business Partner (Transformation), the Planning and Insights teams, and key stakeholders across the business, you will help design and deliver scalable automation solutions across reporting and insight generation, supporting commercial forecasting and other critical business processes to enable better decision-making.

What you’ll do..

Support the strategy and delivery of the Finance transformation roadmap.

Take a hands-on role in delivering finance automation initiatives, from identifying opportunities through to building and implementing workflow solutions.

Transform manual, Google Sheets-based processes into automated, scalable, and reliable ways of working.

Bring a solutions-focused mindset to problem-solving, challenging existing processes and identifying smarter, more efficient ways to support Finance and the wider business.

Help implement, embed, and continuously improve planning and forecasting processes.

Design and build automation workflows and financial models that streamline data flows across systems and support commercial initiatives and decision-making.

Collaborate with the Planning and Insights teams to enhance forecast accuracy and support self-service reporting, analysis, and insight generation through automated data pipelines.

About you…  

Strong problem-solving skills, supported by a structured, analytical, and logical approach.

2+ years’ experience in a technology consulting, finance automation, or technical business analyst role is highly desirable. 

Intermediate SQL skills, with the ability to query, manipulate, and interpret data to support reporting, analysis and automation.

Experience with low-code workflow automation tools such as n8n, and other AI tools is advantageous, though not essential. Curiosity, adaptability and a willingness to learn are essential.

Comfortable working with ambiguity and translating business needs into practical finance solutions.

Strong capability in using reporting, forecasting and financial modelling tools to support business decision-making.

Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build effective stakeholder relationships.

A continuous improvement mindset, with the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.

Why you'll love THE ICONIC..

From our ways of working to our growth mindset and sustainable approach, we each add unique value and have an incredible amount of fun doing it! People are our greatest asset and our ICONITE experience is designed to empower you to do your best work. Here’s what to expect when you join THE ICONIC:

Flexible working. We offer a hybrid and flexible working model so you can do your best work in a way that works for you

The learning  collective. Get your knowledge fix with our learning days and hackathons

Parents at THE ICONIC. Access to our parental leave program and an extra day off for your kids' first day of school every year

Classification:  Academic Level D
Salary package: $174,482 - $185,334 per annum plus 17% superannuation
Terms: Full-time, Continuing

Position Description and Selection Criteria: PD & PEWER_Director,Australian Data Archive_Academic Level D.pdf

Position Overview

This is an exciting leadership opportunity as the Director of the Australian Data Archive (ADA), hosted by POLIS: The Centre for Social Policy Research at The Australian National University. In this role, you will lead the ADA as a national research data infrastructure service within POLIS, collaborating with partners across the University, Australian universities, government agencies and international standards organisations. The occupant of this position will be required to successfully manage the day-to-day operations of ADA at the local and national levels. The role includes responsibility for ADA data archiving processes, including archive and access activities, and coordination with the international organisations to support standards and tools which the Archive needs to maintain in its services. In addition, the occupant will coordinate ADA’s operational requirements in the development, design and implementation of ADA procedures for management of the ADA collection.

The Centre

The Australian Data Archive (ADA) provides a national service for the collection, preservation and dissemination of digital research data, enabling secure and sustainable reuse across social science and related domains.  The ADA is based in POLIS: The Centre for Social Policy Research which is designed to provide a designated space at the University for discussion, debate and research on the formulation of social policy. The Centre provides research and expertise on social policy in response to community and federal and state/territory government needs and requirements. POLIS delivers exceptionally robust data and evidence driven insights into the key challenges facing contemporary Australia. This provides the foundational cornerstones of informed social policy development amongst leading stakeholders within our modern policy landscape: government, community groups, business representatives, and educators.

The ANU College of Arts and Social Sciences (CASS) is one of seven Colleges at the University. The College, which is structured into two main research schools, offers degrees in more than 20 discipline areas and excels in research across the creative arts, humanities and social sciences. The College has a substantial international research presence and is a major source of national policy advice. Our academic staff are internationally recognised for their research, and 57 are members of the Australian Academy of the Humanities, the Academy of the Social Sciences of Australia, or both. We also host 13 Australian Research Council Future Fellows and three ARC Laureates. A hub of vibrant activity, we host more than 270 lectures, concerts and exhibitions each year, most of which are open to the public. Our students, staff and graduates come from more than 60 nations, bringing a diversity of perspective to campus life.

For enquiries regarding this role, please contact Professor Ben Edwards, E: Ben.Edwards@anu.edu.au

The University actively encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. For more information on employment opportunities, contact our Indigenous Employment Consultant on ind••••••••••••••••••@anu.edu.au 

ANU values diversity and inclusion and is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to those of all backgrounds and identities. For more information about staff equity at ANU, visit https://services.anu.edu.au/human-resources/respect-inclusion

About this role
As the Senior Exposure Management Analyst, you will own the operation of nib's Exposure Management function as a durable Line 1 capability.

The role is accountable for continuously identifying, prioritising, escalating, and tracking remediation of technology exposures, and validating that remediation is effective through retesting/verification. This includes unifying exposure signals across vulnerability management and posture sources into a consistent, risk‑aligned remediation view that supports operational risk reduction and regulatory obligations.

About you
We're looking for a security professional with strong experience in Exposure and Vulnerability Management, who can confidently drive end-to-end operations across identification, prioritisation, and remediation.

You bring a hands-on approach to managing vulnerabilities at scale-coordinating remediation efforts across teams, escalating where needed, and ensuring issues are effectively resolved through validation and retesting. Your ability to maintain momentum and oversight across the lifecycle will be key to strengthening our security posture.

You apply a risk-based mindset to everything you do, going beyond standard scoring models to assess exposure in context. Leveraging threat intelligence and real-world risk signals, you're able to prioritise what truly matters-focusing effort on the vulnerabilities that pose the greatest impact to the business.

You'll bring:

Relevant degree qualification in a related discipline (e.g., cybersecurity, information technology, computer science) and/or equivalent industry experience
Experience operating remediation tracking and reporting cadences with multiple technology teams, including planning cycles and stakeholder communication
Have / are working towards information security industry certification(s). Beneficial certifications may include ISC2 certifications, SANS/GIAC, and/or ISO/IEC 27001 certifications (preferred)
Working understanding of privacy and data protection obligations (e.g., GDPR awareness) (preferred)
Experience improving or enabling operational security reporting (e.g., tooling-driven reporting, posture metrics, remediation tracking or governance reporting enablement) (preferred)
Experience contributing to security capability maturity uplift activities such as technical workshops, campaigns, or continuous improvement initiatives (preferred)

Data Lead

We are seeking a dynamic and highly technical Data Lead to spearhead data engineering and analytics initiatives for an enterprise client. In this role, you will bridge the gap between complex enterprise systems and modern cloud architecture, turning raw data into actionable business intelligence.
You will lead, mentor, and scale a talented team of Data Engineers and Data Analysts. Operating within a fast-paced landscape, you will oversee the design, implementation, and optimisation of robust ETL/ELT pipelines, ensuring data streams are seamless, accurate, and highly scalable.

Team Leadership & Strategy
People Management: Lead, mentor, and foster the professional growth of a high-performing team of Data Engineers and Data Analysts.
Resource Allocation: Manage sprint planning, prioritise project backlogs, and align team deliverables with broader business objectives.
Data Governance: Establish and enforce best practices for data modeling, code quality, testing, and data security across the organisation.
Architecture & Data Engineering
Enterprise Integration: Architect and oversee ingestion pipelines from major enterprise systems (e.g. SAP ERP, CRM) into the central data ecosystem.
Cloud Infrastructure: Drive the evolution of cloud data, utilising Microsoft Azure or GCP services to build scalable, secure solutions.
Data Pipelines: Design, optimise, and maintain high-volume ETL/ELT workflows to support real-time and batch processing requirements.
Analytics & Business Enablement
Insights: Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver deep analytical insights 
Self-Service Analytics: Empower business units by designing clean, optimised data models (e.g., Star Schema) that fuel intuitive dashboards and reporting tools.
Technical Skills & Experience
Industry Focus: Ideally 10+ years of data experience, with a significant portion spent within fast paced environments (e.g. FMCG, Retail, Supply Chain/Logistics) 
Leadership: 3+ years of experience formally managing or leading a technical team of engineers and analysts.
Enterprise Systems: Strong hands-on familiarity working with SAP (e.g., SAP BW, S/4HANA) or equivalent enterprise-scale ERP data structures.
Technical Stack Proficiencies
Cloud Platform: Proven expertise in Microsoft Azure or GCP data components (e.g., Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Synapse, Azure Data Lake Gen2, GCP Pub/Sub, BigQuery, GCS, Dataform/dbt, Looker, Composer).
Data Processing: Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python for complex data manipulation.
Data Warehousing & Tools: Deep understanding of modern ETL/ELT frameworks, data orchestration (e.g. Airflow, dbt), and data lakehouse architectures (Delta Lake).
BI & Analytics: Familiarity with visualisation tools like Power BI or Tableau to guide the analytics team on delivery.
This is an awesome opportunity to work on a greenfield program with plenty of autonomy to really drive home what the data architecture and engineering roadmap looks like. If you're interested, please apply now!

Forward with Cuscal.  

Job Description


As part of a conditional, binding agreement to acquire another Australian payment services provider, subject to regulatory approvals, we are looking for a Senior Project Analyst to join our Program team. 

As the Senior Project Analyst you will, provide oversight and support for the effective delivery of the project workstream’s outputs and products, ensuring that each project stream within the broader program is executed successfully.  

You’ll be responsible for driving the achievement of strategic objectives and benefits, maintaining delivery within budget, on schedule, and to the required standard of quality, in alignment with Cuscal’s Project Management and Project Investment Governance and Execution Frameworks.   

You’ll also make an impact by:  

Maintain integration governance with clear controls to manage progress, risks, issues, dependencies, assumptions and resources.  
Coordinate integration tasks with Program outcomes—synergy, culture, and operations—under direction from the Program Director and Integration Lead.  
Provide a consistent and precise overview of integration progress, enabling effective change management and informed decision-making regarding legacy systems, processes, and priorities.  
Apply best practice project governance frameworks appropriate to the Program and ensure data hygiene across program management systems e.g JIRA, Confluence.  
Collaborate with both internal and external teams to develop plans aligned with the agreed scope of work and manage stakeholders and their expectations.  
Manage executive reporting cadence to track integration milestones, synergy targets, and risk indicators using RAG status and clear stakeholder communication (as needed and applicable to the workstream).  
Ensure adherence to project governance frameworks, templates, and methodologies to drive quality delivery.  
Support Program budget management, including synergy tracking, transition costs, forecasting, contingency and resource utilization to ensure alignment with financial plans.  
Review business cases, funding proposals, and change requests for completeness and alignment with scope, schedule, and resource needs 

Qualifications


What you’ll bring  

You’ve got an inquisitive nature with the ability to see the bigger picture; you’ve got excellent communication skills and are detail orientated. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are passionate about Agile ways of working.   

To succeed in this role, you’ll have:  

5+ years of experience performing end to end project governance (PMO) of complex initiatives, covering business, technology and vendor engagement involving multiple industry stakeholders and multi delivery streams of work  
Experience of structured project methodologies, tools and recognised project management disciplines overseeing quality assurance and change processes and practises.  
Exceptional organisation skills and are skilled in influencing, guiding and facilitating stakeholders and peers with decision making  
Tertiary education/degree equivalent and have Prince2/PMP or equivalent  

Although not required the following experience would be highly desirable:  

Experience with mergers and acquisitions, or in fast-growing environment.   
Financial Services experience in payments and banking.  

Additional Information


Why Cuscal? 

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all.   

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves.  

You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. 

And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including: 

Recognition and growth: Celebrate achievements through IGNITE and grow with tailored development opportunities. 
Wellbeing focus: Access initiatives supporting physical, mental, and financial health, plus discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.’ 
Diversity and inclusion: Join a workplace that values different perspectives and flexible work arrangements. 

Join us and reimagine the future 

Ready to make an impact? Click APPLY to join today. 

If you’re excited about this opportunity, we’d love to explore how you can contribute to our vision for the future. Screening and interviews may occur before the job ad closing date, so don’t wait - apply now.

Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions.

Job Description


We are supporting a major defence programme in Adelaide, delivering a complex, long‑term portfolio of work of national significance. The programme requires strong governance, data‑driven decision‑making and clear performance insight to enable successful outcomes. This role sits at the heart of programme governance and reporting.

The Reporting Manager is responsible for delivering high‑quality, timely and consistent performance reporting and insights across a very large, complex programme of work. The role supports governance, strategic decision‑making and proactive performance management.

Key responsibilities include:

Develop, maintain and publish monthly dashboards and reports for all sub‑programmes within the reporting system.
Operate and continuously improve reporting systems to ensure data is accurate, timely and actionable.
Provide clear, insightful and tailored reporting to senior stakeholders, enabling evidence‑based decisions on performance and deliverability.
Work across cross functional teams, including with Reporting & Systems teams, embedding best practice in data visualisation, analysis and reporting.
Delivers to the reporting calendar whilst also fostering a forward-looking ethos at reporting trends, hotspots to enable preventative intervention
Ensure the reporting delivered is fact based, aligning stakeholders to one single source of truth
Have the ability to spot problems before they are problems and identify unreported risks and issues – filter through reporting noise to identify important signals

Qualifications


To be successful in this role, you will bring a blend of technical reporting expertise, leadership capability and stakeholder influence.

Essential qualifications and experience:

Degree (or equivalent) in Business, Finance, Engineering, Commercial, Project Management or a related discipline.
Proven experience delivering reporting and insights for large, complex programmes or portfolios.
Strong capability in performance measurement frameworks, reporting hierarchies and governance reporting.
Advanced data analysis and visualisation skills, with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights.
Demonstrated experience influencing and engaging senior stakeholders across multidisciplinary teams.

Desirable:

APM qualification (or equivalent) and experience across P3M environments.
Substantial experience with Power BI or equivalent reporting tools.
Experience within defence, utilities, water or other regulated industries.
Background in data quality management and continuous improvement initiatives.

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - AI Enablement (AU remote)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. 

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney, with a second campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth, & Adelaide. You have flexibility in how and where you work — whether that's from one of our spaces, from home, or a mix of both. This role is remote-friendly within Australia, so you can choose the setup that empowers you and your team to do your best work.

Job Description


About the Group/Team

Canva's AI Platform Group serves as the foundation for AI innovation across the company, and achieving our GenAI ambitions depends on its ability to scale effectively. Within this group, the AI Enablement teams provide critical solution expertise to researchers and AI builders globally, helping them navigate platform capabilities, apply expert domain knowledge, optimise implementations, and accelerate the journey from experimentation to production. These teams bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and reliable, production-ready features, ensuring that innovation translates into real user value.

About the Role/Specialty

Please note, this is not a model building role

This role sits at the intersection of solution expertise and platform engineering, requiring deep technical expertise across ML domains combined with strong platform knowledge. The senior MLE will context-switch between facilitating model evaluations, data pipeline, optimising inference performance and working directly with product teams building solutions.

The nature of enablement work demands someone who operates as a force multiplier, finding satisfaction not just in solving technical problems but in enabling others to move faster. The role operates in a high-velocity environment where priorities shift rapidly based on team needs, requiring both technical excellence and pragmatic judgment. This combination of deep expertise and adaptive problem-solving is critical to accelerating the delivery of GenAI capabilities across Canva, removing friction from the path to production and ensuring product teams across the company can ship AI-powered features at the pace and quality our ambitions require.

What you’ll do (responsibilities):


Responsibilities will evolve as Canva's needs change, but here's what the role will focus on initially:

Act as a solution expert across ML domains including evaluations, training, inference, data pipelines, labelling, and optimisation

Work directly alongside product teams as a trusted partner, helping them navigate technical challenges and arrive at effective solutions

Provide expert guidance on platform capabilities, helping teams understand what's possible and how to get there efficiently

Develop blueprints, patterns, and paved roads that allow other teams to follow proven approaches and accelerate their own implementations

Debug and resolve complex issues, identifying root causes and sharing learnings broadly

Balance hands-on problem solving with strategic thinking about how to scale enablement impact across the organisation

You're probably a match if you have any of the following:

Strong Python expertise with experience in ML frameworks

Experience working with Kubernetes

Experience or knowledge of building data pipelines

Experience or knowledge of model evaluations

Experience with inference optimisation and serving infrastructure

Experience supporting or working closely with research, data science, or product teams

Excellent debugging and problem-solving skills across the full ML stack

Ability to context-switch effectively across multiple concurrent projects

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to varied audiences

A collaborative mindset with a focus on enabling others to succeed

Additional Information


Don't tick all the boxes? Don't worry about that - nobody does!  

We’d still love to hear from you! At Canva, we know that great engineers come from a variety of backgrounds, and we value passion, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as specific experience. If you're excited about this role but don’t tick every box, we encourage you to apply, you might a great fit in ways you didn’t expect!

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out  lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

All interviews are conducted virtually

Location: This role could be based out of any of our PwC offices in Australia




About us




At PwC, our people in risk and compliance focus on maintaining regulatory compliance and managing risks for clients, providing advice, and solutions. They help organisations navigate complex regulatory landscapes and enhance their internal controls to mitigate risks effectively.

In Actuarial services at PwC, you will be responsible for analysing and managing financial risks for clients through statistical modelling and data analysis. Your work will generate valuable insights and recommendations to help businesses make informed decisions and mitigate potential risks.




Why PwC?




At PwC Australia, your skills meet purpose. We tackle big challenges across industries like finance, technology, energy, and health—giving you the chance to make a real impact. Here, your growth is our priority. You’ll work with leading teams, explore new technologies, and unlock your full potential.




Join a global community of more than 370,000 people who value bold ideas, collaboration, and lasting change. Together, we’re building trust and shaping the future.




What you’ll do:

Analyse and identify the linkages and interactions between the component parts of an entire system.

Take ownership of projects, ensuring their successful planning, budgeting, execution, and completion.

Partner with team leadership to ensure collective ownership of quality, timelines, and deliverables.

Develop skills outside your comfort zone and encourage others to do the same.

Effectively mentor others.

Use the review of work as an opportunity to deepen the expertise of team members.

Address conflicts or issues, engaging in difficult conversations with clients, team members and other stakeholders, escalating where appropriate.

Uphold and reinforce professional and technical standards (e.g. refer to specific PwC tax and audit guidance), the Firm's code of conduct, and independence requirements.




What we’re looking for:

Practical exposure to prudential regulatory requirements and their implementation;

Excellent analytical and problem solving skills and enjoy working with clients to support on a range of projects;

Strong communication, project management and stakeholder management skills;

Working well in teams and can support and develop more junior team members in a high-performing and caring work environment; and

You hold a MSc / BSc (Hons) in Statistics / Actuarial Science / Financial Mathematics or Applied Mathematics as well as have programming capabilities in SAS / R / Python / SQL




What you could gain:

The opportunity to work with leading organisations and cutting-edge technology that challenge and expand your expertise.

Flexibility means trusting you to choose when, where, and how you work—balancing what’s best for you, your team, and your clients to grow and thrive together.

Clear paths for career progression supported by continuous learning and leadership development.

Generous leave entitlements including four weeks’ annual leave and float public holidays to support your work-life balance.




We know that perks are as important as your financial rewards. Explore all the benefits that PwC has to offer here: https://bit.ly/4fFLA3z







Ready to grow here and go further? Join PwC Australia as an Actuarial Professional and make an impact that goes beyond the expected. Apply now and take your next step with a team shaping the future.




The minimum salary for a Senior Associate in this role is AUD 89,100 including superannuation. Higher grades will receive a salary range above this in line with the salary bands for these positions.  




We’re committed to treating all our job applicants fairly and with respect, irrespective of their actual or assumed background, disability, neurodivergence, or any other protected characteristic. We want you to have every opportunity to thrive in our selection process. In the application form, you can let us know what adjustments you require during our recruitment process and/or any workplace accommodations you anticipate needing to help you perform your role. 




No agencies please: We kindly request that recruitment agencies do not submit CVs in response to this advertisement. We are only accepting applications direct from individuals.
